name: Basic Cider
recipeIngredient: 2 Packages Brewer's Yeast - EDME or Cooper
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 1 1/2 gallons apple juice to a simmer in a large pot.
2. Add brown sugar and stir constantly until completely dissolved.
3. Remove from heat.
4. Add yeast extract and ascorbic acid, stirring well.
5. Pour warm sugared apple juice into a fermenter (5 or 6 gallon glass carboy or 6 gallon plastic bucket with tight-sealing lid).
6. Rehydrate the yeast according to package directions.
7. When the juice reaches 75° F, add rehydrated yeast and pectic enzyme to the fermenter and stir gently to mix.
8. Seal the fermenter by placing the lid on the bucket or cork in the carboy opening.
9. Attach an airlock through the hole in the cork or plastic lid.
10. Store the fermenter in a cool place away from direct sunlight.
11. Fermentation may take 4-10 days.
12. When no longer bubbling, transfer the liquid to another sealable container (preferably glass), leaving as much sediment as possible.
13. Allow cider to age in a dark cool place for 4-6 months or longer before bottling.
14. Enjoy!
